title: Pilosella substoloniflora

Pilosella substoloniflora is native to parts of Europe and is known for its yellow dandelion-like flowers. The species is also known as Pilosella flabellifolia in some regions. intro: Pilosella substoloniflora, also known as a type of dandelion, is a perennial plant in the Asteraceae family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and skin problems due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella substoloniflora include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosella substoloniflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support liver and kidney function, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
